question: How does FMLA leave differ from personal leave?
answer:
According to our policies, FMLA leave differs from personal leave in the following ways:

- FMLA leave is granted for specific reasons such as the birth of a child, adoption, foster care, caring for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition, or military family leave (Source 2 | Policy: Federal Family and Medical Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk).
- FMLA leave is unpaid, but employees can substitute vacation days according to the schedule (Source 3 | Policy: Family and Medical Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk).
- FMLA leave allows for intermittent leave and can be taken in a non-continuous manner (Source 4 | Policy: Family and Medical Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk).
- FMLA leave maintains group health plan coverage and certain other employment benefits during the leave period (Source 4 | Policy: Family and Medical Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk).

On the other hand, personal leave is not granted for employment outside of VanaciPrime, pursuing an independent business venture, or as additional leave after FMLA (or Non-FMLA) Medical Leave (Source 1 | Policy: Personal Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk). Personal leave is not allowed to be taken intermittently (Source 1 | Policy: Personal Leave Policy | Section: Time Away From Work | Type: policy_chunk).
contexts:
Leave approval or denial is done at the discretion of the employee’s supervisor and Human Resources. Personal leave is
not granted for engaging in employment outside of , pursuing an independent business venture or as additional leave
after FMLA (or Non-FMLA) Medical Leave. This leave policy does not allow for intermittent leave.
[EMPLOYER: It is advised that FMLA leave rights be considered and offered first when the employee is FMLA-covered
and eligible.]
Pay Status

Amount of Leave Available
Eligible employees may take up to a total of 12 weeks of FMLA leave within a rolling 12-month period, measured
backward from the date an employee uses any FMLA leave, for any combination of the following reasons:
o
The birth of an employee’s newborn child or the placement of a child with the employee for adoption or foster
care
o
To care for the employee’s spouse, child or parent with a serious health condition
o

the covered service member, to substantiate your need for FMLA leave.
Substituting Paid Leave for Unpaid Leave
Federal FMLA leave is unpaid. The Company requires you to substitute vacation days according to the schedule below.

approves a non-continuous leave for the birth of a child or the placement of a child for adoption or foster care.
Benefit Continuation during Leave
The Company will maintain your group health plan coverage and certain other employment benefits (such as group life
insurance, AD&D insurance and health and dependent flexible spending accounts) during your FMLA leave on the same
terms as if you had continued to work, if these benefits were provided to you before the leave was taken. You will be

unable to perform daily activities, the need for hospitalization or continuing treatment by a health care provider, or circumstances supporting the
need for military family leave. Employees also must inform the employer if the requested leave is for a reason for which FMLA leave was previously
taken or certified. Employees also may be required to provide a certification and periodic recertification supporting the need for leave.
Employer Responsibilities
ground_truth: Federal FMLA provides up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per rolling 12 months for eligible employees (12 months of service, 1,250 hours, and other eligibility rules) with required notices and certification. Company personal leave is discretionary, may be limited to a stated maximum in the template, generally requires 12 months of service and good standing, and does not guarantee job restoration.

ground_truth: The Drug-free Workplace Policy prohibits unlawful use, possession, and being under the influence of alcohol or illegal drugs on the job or company premises. The Drug and Alcohol Testing Policy describes when testing occurs (pre-employment, periodic, random, reasonable suspicion, post-accident, follow-up) and how positive tests and refusals are handled.

question: How do bereavement leave and personal days differ?
answer: I don't have enough information in our HR policies to answer that question.
contexts:
has taken into consideration the personal needs that arise from the death of an immediate family member. In the event
of such a loss, an employee will be allowed up to three days of leave with full pay until and including the day of the
funeral. Funeral leave will not count against accrued paid time off (PTO), vacation or sick leave. Funeral leave pay will not
be granted to employees attending a funeral during periods when they are not at work for other reasons, such as
vacation, holidays and illness.
Immediate family includes: a father, mother, spouse, child, sister, brother, mother-in-law, father-in-law, brother-in-law,
sister-in-law, grandparent or any relative who lives with the employee.
Employees should notify their supervisor of the need to use funeral leave. Within a reasonable period of time, the
employee may be required to provide verification of need, such as an obituary.

incapacity due to a chronic condition. Other conditions may meet the definition of continuing treatment.

permits and your supervisor approves.
Sick time is a benefit provided to you in the event you need to take time off because of your health or the health of a

Other Types of Leave
If you do not qualify for the types of leave described in this policy, the Company may approve a personal leave of
absence, depending on your circumstances. Except where mandated by law, we cannot guarantee that benefits will
continue or that your position will remain open in your absence.

does not constitute an accepted notification of absence. If you do not report for work and VanaciPrime is not notified of your status,
it will be assumed after two consecutive days of absence that you have voluntarily resigned, and you will be removed
from the payroll.
If you become ill at work or must leave the office for some other reason before the end of the workday, be sure to
inform your supervisor of the situation.
ground_truth: Funeral leave provides up to three paid days for the death of an immediate family member (as defined in the policy) and does not count against PTO, vacation, or sick leave. There is no separate handbook “personal days” bucket; general personal needs are typically addressed through PTO (capped at 40 accrued hours).

The Standards of Conduct / sexual harassment provisions address unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and sexual conduct, with emphasis on reporting to Human Resources.

question: How does the disciplinary action process differ from immediate termination?
answer:
According to the Disciplinary Action Policy (Source 1 | Policy: Disciplinary Action Policy | Section: Workplace Conduct | Type: policy_chunk), disciplinary actions may entail verbal, written, and final warnings, suspensions, and termination. However, prior warning is not a requirement for termination.

Immediate termination is mentioned in the Disciplinary Action Policy (Source 5 | Policy: Disciplinary Action Policy | Section: Workplace Conduct | Type: policy_chunk) as a possible outcome in certain situations, such as discourtesy to a customer, vendor, or the general public, refusal or failure to follow directions from management, insubordination, breach of confidentiality, and others.

The key difference between the disciplinary action process and immediate termination is that disciplinary actions typically involve a series of steps (verbal, written, and final warnings, suspensions) before reaching termination, whereas immediate termination is a more severe and direct outcome, often resulting from serious misconduct or breaches of company policies.
contexts:
Disciplinary actions may entail verbal, written and final warnings, suspensions and termination. Not all of these actions
may be followed in all instances. reserves the right to exercise discretion in discipline. Prior warning is not a requirement
for termination. All disciplinary actions will be documented; documentation will be placed in personnel files.
reserves the right to take any disciplinary action the company considers appropriate, including termination, at any time.

ground_truth: Discipline may include verbal, written, and final warnings, suspensions, and termination, but prior warning is not always required. Immediate termination may apply for listed examples including insubordination, dishonesty, fighting, policy violations, serious performance or conduct issues, or failing a drug or alcohol test.

question: If I exhaust all my PTO and sick leave, what options do I have for additional time off?
answer: I don't have enough information in our HR policies to answer that question.
contexts:
believes that employees should have opportunities to enjoy time away from work to help balance their lives. For this
reason, we provide a Paid Time Off (PTO) program to all full-time (if applicable: and part-time) employees.
PTO provides employees the freedom to decide how to use their personal time off. believes this program offers more
generous time off with pay than traditional vacation, sick and personal time packages. Employees can use their PTO days

who are too injured or ill to work, not as additional personal or vacation time. Employees should be prepared to furnish a
doctor’s note or similar evidence of inability to work if the supervisor requests one. Abuse of sick leave is grounds for
discipline, up to and including termination.
Extended Leave
Employees who require three or more days off due their own health issue or a health issue of a family member may be

needs at the time. understands there may be occasions, such as sudden illness, when you may not be able to give
sufficient advance notice. In those situations, however, be sure to inform your supervisor as soon as possible.
PTO also includes time off for unexpected emergencies or illness. Do not use PTO to cover time missed from work due to
tardiness, except in the case of severe weather.
Types of Non-PTO Leave

Paid time off accrual will restart upon the employee's return to paid status.
Employee Benefits
While an employee is on approved leave, will continue the employee's health benefits at the same level and under the
same conditions as if the employee had continued to work, as long as the employee continues to pay a portion of the
health care premium.
While on paid leave, the employer will continue to make payroll deductions to collect the employee's share of the
premium.

policy to comply with a paid sick leave law, the policy must generally meet the minimum requirements for accrual and
usage, as provided under the applicable paid sick leave law.
ground_truth: You may request FMLA leave if eligible (up to 12 weeks unpaid for qualifying reasons). You might also request non-FMLA medical leave or personal leave depending on circumstances; personal leave requires exhausting accrued paid time first and does not guarantee job restoration.

ground_truth: PTO accrual begins after 3 months of employment, and no paid vacation days are granted until the third month of employment is completed under the Vacation Policy. During the first 90-day orientation period, performance is monitored and termination is possible for unsatisfactory performance, so scheduling extended paid time off early on is unlikely and may not align with accrual rules.

Worker's Compensation and Non-FMLA Medical Leave
Non-FMLA Medical Leave and Worker's Compensation can run concurrently.

Returning to Work
If the reason for FMLA leave is for your own serious health condition, you will be required to present a fitness-for-duty

Substitution of Paid Leave for Unpaid Leave
Employees may choose or employers may require use of accrued paid leave while taking FMLA leave. In order to use paid leave for FMLA leave,

ground_truth: Yes. Federal FMLA leave is unpaid, but the company requires substitution of vacation/PTO according to the FMLA policy’s substitution schedule; substituted paid leave runs concurrently with FMLA and does not extend the leave entitlement.

question: If I am terminated, do I get paid for unused vacation days?
answer: According to our Employment Termination Policy (Section: Employment Policies), unused vacation pay will be paid on a pro-rata basis in the case of termination due to resignation, retirement, or a permanent reduction in the work force. However, if you are terminated for any other reason, any vacation or personal/sick time used in excess of accrued time will be deducted from your final paycheck.
